facebook.texto: You didn't fail the diet. The diet never addressed what was actually broken. ⠀ Because if your metabolism's signaling is off — hunger doesn't stop when it should, blood sugar spikes and crashes all day, and your body holds onto fat like a famine is coming — no calorie deficit fixes that. ⠀ This is the part the weight loss industry skips. ⠀ And it's why millions of people are eating well, moving their bodies, doing everything "right" — and nothing moves. ⠀

analise_criativa.avatar: White woman with red/blonde hair, appears to be in her 40s, filmed outdoors in a backyard setting wearing a black mini dress and white boots. Shows a before/after body transformation across frames, with a middle frame showing her in the kitchen with a cooking appliance — suggesting a recipe or method element.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical TikTok-style UGC video with natural lighting and casual handheld feel. On-screen text overlays used to mark transformation milestones ('after two weeks, 15 lbs', 'I'm 45'). Raw, unpolished aesthetic consistent with authentic user-generated content.
analise_criativa.observation: The ad copy is positioned as a metabolic/hormonal angle ('metabolism signaling is off') which contrasts with the before/after visual format — this mismatch between copy angle and visual format may dilute message clarity. The kitchen frame at 15s introduces a possible 'recipe trick' hook but is not clearly connected to the transformation narrative.
analise_criativa.highlight: The '45 years old, 15 lbs in two weeks' on-screen text combined with a relatable, non-model woman in a casual backyard setting is a strong scroll-stopper for the 40+ female target demographic. Age disclosure ('I'm 45') is a proven trust signal for this audience. The before/after body reveal in natural clothing (not gym wear) feels authentic and aspirational simultaneously.

facebook.texto: GLP-1 has become one of the biggest wellness trends in recent years 💭 Why? Because GLP-1 is a natural hormone involved in appetite regulation, feelings of fullness, and metabolic function. But here's what many people don't realize: Your daily habits, diet, and gut health can all play a role in supporting your body's natural GLP-1 activity. That's why more people are exploring ways to support their wellness routines without injections. Bioma GLP-1 is formulated with science-backed ingredients des

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman with fuller body taking a mirror selfie with a man in the background (frame 1); two women standing in an elevator smiling (frame 2); dark-haired woman speaking to camera from inside a car (frame 3)
analise_criativa.editing: Casual, authentic UGC style with minimal editing — vertical format, natural lighting, no heavy post-production. Text overlays visible on frames. Multi-scene narrative structure moving from personal moment to testimonial delivery.
analise_criativa.observation: Three distinct scenes suggest a compilation or multi-person UGC format. The mirror selfie opening is a strong scroll-stopper as it implies a before/after or body-awareness moment. The car talking-head segment is the most direct persuasion layer.
analise_criativa.highlight: The mirror selfie opener with a visibly fuller-bodied woman is highly relatable and scroll-stopping for the target audience. The car segment adds trust via direct-to-camera delivery. Multi-avatar approach may help with broad audience testing.

Because GLP-1 is a natural hormone involved in appetite regulation, feelings of fullness, and metabolic function. That's why more people are exploring ways to support their wellness routines without injections. Bioma GLP-1 is formulated with science-backed ingredients des

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman with fuller body taking a mirror selfie with a man in the background (frame 1); two women standing in an elevator smiling (frame 2); dark-haired woman speaking to camera from inside a car (frame 3)
analise_criativa.editing: Casual, authentic UGC style with minimal editing — vertical format, natural lighting, no heavy post-production. Text overlays visible on frames. Multi-scene narrative structure moving from personal moment to testimonial delivery.
analise_criativa.observation: Identical creative to AD 1 — same three frames, same ad text, only the page name differs (Jen Creates UGC with Bioma.Health vs unknown page). This is a duplicate test running under a different page/avatar name. 11 days active, same as AD 1.
analise_criativa.highlight: Running the same creative under different page identities is a classic DR split-test for social proof and audience segmentation. Worth monitoring which page variant drives better CTR. The mirror selfie opener remains the strongest hook element.

facebook.texto: GLP-1 has become one of the biggest wellness trends in recent years 💭 Why? Because GLP-1 is a natural hormone involved in appetite regulation, feelings of fullness, and metabolic function. But here's what many people don't realize: Your daily habits, diet, and gut health can all play a role in supporting your body's natural GLP-1 activity. That's why more people are exploring ways to support their wellness routines without injections. Bioma GLP-1 is formulated with science-backed ingredients des

analise_criativa.avatar: Young woman filmed from behind on a beach in a bikini, lying on a towel — implies body confidence or transformation context. Later frames shift to dark dramatic visuals and a discount CTA screen.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical mobile-first video. Frame 1 is bright, outdoor lifestyle beach shot. Frame 2 shifts to dark, high-contrast dramatic tone with text overlay. Frame 3 is a dark CTA screen with bold neon-green '35% OFF' text and 'START TODAY' button — abrupt tonal shift between frames.
analise_criativa.observation: The beach frame at 3s has no explicit weight loss signal on its own — relies heavily on copy and sequence to build context. The tonal jump from warm lifestyle to dark/dramatic to CTA is jarring and may feel disconnected. No face shown, which reduces personal connection.
analise_criativa.highlight: Beach body opener is a proven scroll-stopper for weight loss audiences. The 'natural Ozempic' keyword alignment with GLP-1 copy is a strong trend-riding hook. The 35% OFF urgency close could drive clicks if earlier frames build sufficient curiosity.

facebook.texto: This is how I regained the confidence I had when I was young after years of being overweight. I want to tell you about the moment I realized something had fundamentally changed. I was at a restaurant I'd been going to for fifteen years. Same maitre d'. Same table they used to hold for me without being asked. The kind of place where the sommelier remembers your name and the chef sends out a little something extra just because you walked in. I stood at the host stand for four minutes.

analise_criativa.avatar: Middle-aged woman (Megan Hartwell) shown in a casual kitchen setting in her profile photo — positioned as a real person sharing a personal weight loss confidence story.
analise_criativa.editing: Static image ad. The creative uses a screenshot-style format showing a Facebook post with embedded social proof (comments, likes visible). Feels organic and native to the feed. No heavy design or branding overlays.
analise_criativa.observation: The actual ad image appears to be a screenshot of a social media post with engagement visible — a common trust-building native format. The destination URL (shopledisa.com) and headline 'I Used To Stop Traffic. Then I Turned 50 and Gained 30 lbs' suggest a VSL funnel. GLP-1 angle referenced in subtext.
analise_criativa.highlight: The copy hook is exceptionally strong — restaurant scene storytelling builds curiosity and emotional identification instantly. 'Regained the confidence I had when I was young' is a highly relatable trigger for the 45-60 female demographic. Social proof screenshot format increases native feel and credibility. Worth testing copy angle as a talking head video.

facebook.texto: Ozempic is way out of my budget so I've been researching natural alternatives. Has anyone lost weight with GLP-1 cutting drinks?

analise_criativa.avatar: Debra Rogers — middle-aged blonde woman visible only in profile picture thumbnail, presenting as a relatable everyday person asking a question in a Facebook post format. The ad image itself appears to show a cartoon/illustration rather than a real person in context.
analise_criativa.editing: Minimal native Facebook post style — no heavy editing, designed to mimic organic social content. Profile picture of a real woman used as credibility anchor. The body of the ad is text-based with a simple illustrative image below.
analise_criativa.observation: The ad image below the text appears to contain a cartoon or diagram element (ladder/funnel illustration) rather than a real person, which weakens visual impact. However, the overall format mimics a genuine Facebook community post/question, which can drive curiosity and comments. The persona 'Debra Rogers' reads as a real person rather than a brand page, adding authenticity.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong social proof mechanic — framing as a question from a real person ('Has anyone lost weight with GLP-1 cutting drinks?') is a classic native DR strategy. The Ozempic budget angle is highly relatable for UK audiences and taps directly into the natural alternatives trend. Worth testing with a stronger visual of a real woman in context.

repeated_creatives.description: Identical ad copy opening with 'GLP-1 has become one of the biggest wellness trends in recent years' and identical avatar descriptions across all three frames (mirror selfie woman, elevator women, car woman) — strongly indicating the same original UGC video reused across multiple page/attribution variations

frequent_avatars.type: Relatable middle-aged woman — everyday person framing, not a fitness influencer, shown in domestic or casual settings (kitchen, car, social media profile thumbnail), positioned as a peer sharing a personal discovery rather than a brand spokesperson

UGC multi-scene woman — fuller-bodied woman shown across multiple candid social contexts (mirror selfie, elevator, car), suggesting an authentic lifestyle journey rather than a polished transformation

facebook_cross_analysis.dominant_mechanism: Natural GLP-1 activation — positioning the supplement as a way to boost the body's own GLP-1 hormone to suppress appetite, regulate blood sugar, and replicate the effects of Ozempic without a prescription
facebook_cross_analysis.emerging_angle: Metabolic signaling framing (Ad 1) — moving beyond 'appetite suppression' to argue the diet itself was never the problem, but rather broken metabolic signaling, which reframes the supplement as a systemic fix rather than a hunger hack; this shame-reversing, mechanistic hook appears only once but carries high emotional and intellectual resonance
facebook_cross_analysis.market_signal: UK and US markets are aggressively testing 'natural Ozempic' positioning by anchoring to GLP-1 as a credible hormone mechanism, using peer-to-peer UGC formats and budget-conscious framing to capture demand from consumers priced out of prescription GLP-1 drugs
